seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Prince Henry Heights and Prince Of Wales. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Prince Henry Heights vs Prince Of Wales: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Prince Henry Heights and Prince Of Wales.
heading: Prince Henry Heights vs Prince Of Wales — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Prince Henry Heights, Queensland and Prince Of Wales, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Prince Of Wales has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Prince Henry Heights (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Prince Henry Heights or Prince Of Wales safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Prince Henry Heights has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Prince Of Wales scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Prince Henry Heights scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Prince Of Wales scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Prince Henry Heights or Prince Of Wales?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Prince Of Wales has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
